source: Elevated RDW can indicate increased red blood cell production variability, which can be seen in early T1D.
Anemia is a common finding in T1D due to chronic inflammation and impaired erythropoiesis.
Low HCT is a sign of anemia, which is common in T1D.
Elevated WBC count can indicate infection or inflammation, which can be associated with T1D.
Thrombocytosis can be seen in T1D due to increased platelet production.
Microcytosis (low MCV) can be a sign of iron deficiency anemia, which can occur in T1D.
Low MCH indicates smaller red blood cells, which can be seen in iron deficiency anemia.
Low MCHc indicates decreased hemoglobin concentration per cell, which can be seen in iron deficiency anemia.
Low RBC count is a sign of anemia, which is common in T1D.
